commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fe27ace367cdd20ae754d93fa3e0b21eba89a99e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds harness validation, harness-aware `run` and `job run` evidence/compliance outputs, job config support, two plugin tools, tests, and documentation.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows documented job harness compliance defaults are loaded but not forwarded, and no-sync remote harness upload runs before the workdir creation block. I did not run the new harness workflow because this review must keep the checkout read-only and the PR lacks real run proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus maintainer judgment on the default plugin tool contract; automated repair should not be requested while the proof gate is unmet.

target.title: Add harness-aware run evidence
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds harness-aware evidence for `crabbox run` and `crabbox job run`: Markdown harness parsing, YAML frontmatter validation, plan/harness hashes, light grounding metadata, and local compliance reports.
- Adds `crabbox harness validate [--json] HARNESS.md` plus job config support for `harness:` defaults in `.crabbox.yaml`.
- Adds two bounded OpenClaw plugin tools behind `allowHarness`: `crabbox_harness_validate` and `crabbox_job_run_with_harness`.
- Documents harness usage, evidence outputs, compliance behavior, plugin schemas, and a generic Harness/Station/Reasoning Station roadmap.

## What The New Pieces Do
- `internal/cli/harness.go`: parses harness Markdown/frontmatter, rejects unknown top-level and nested frontmatter keys, computes hashes, builds grounding JSON, writes remote/local evidence, and renders compliance reports.
- `run` / `job run`: accept `--harness` and `--index none|light`; harness metadata is included in timing JSON and compliance failures can make an otherwise successful command return nonzero.
- Compliance reports: write `harness.md`, `grounding.json`, `compliance-report.md`, and `compliance-report.json` under `.crabbox/runs/<run-or-lease>/`.
- Required evidence: `require_junit` checks parsed JUnit results; `required_artifacts` matches collected evidence by artifact kind, path, basename, proof template, or JUnit result file.
- OpenClaw tools: expose only typed harness validation and harness job-run flows, without raw argv passthrough.

## Review Fixes Included
- Fixed plugin argv order so `crabbox_harness_validate` emits `harness validate --json HARNESS.md`, matching the Go parser.
- Fixed required artifact compliance so unrelated artifacts no longer satisfy all requirements, and JUnit evidence can satisfy `required_artifacts: junit` when results were parsed.
- Fixed compliance ordering so proof artifacts are produced before harness compliance is evaluated, allowing proof evidence to be required.
- Fixed delegated-run harness compliance so delegated execution errors cannot look like a passed harness because of a zero-valued result exit code.
- Fixed docs examples so `job run` flags appear before the job name, removed stale doc markers from touched pages, and made the roadmap language generic.
- Added regression coverage for parser ordering, nested frontmatter typos, artifact matching, JUnit evidence matching, and local harness evidence output/status.

## Verification / Proof
- `go test ./internal/cli` passes.
- `go test ./...` passes.
- `npm run check` passes: plugin syntax check plus 61 Node tests.
- `npm run docs:check` passes: 42 command docs checked, 139 markdown files checked, docs site built.

The evidence-writer regression test creates a harness evidence directory, verifies all four evidence files are written, checks returned harness metadata, and confirms missing required artifacts produce a failed compliance report with the specific missing item.

## Notes
- The plugin surface is additive and gated by `allowHarness`; maintainers can still decide whether to accept the new tool contract as part of this PR or split it later.
- Harnesses deliberately do not add LLM judging, vector indexes, scenario databases, autonomous state machines, or a remote daemon.
